creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
> How can I correct the keyboard mappings for my system?

The single-sentence paragraph under <https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-doc/blob/main/documentation/content/en/books/faq/_index.adoc#107-how-can-i-correct-the-keyboard-mappings-for-my-system> | <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/faq/#keyboard-mappings>: 

> Refer to the Handbook section on using localization, specifically the 
> section on console setup.

– both links are broken: 

    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/#using-localization>

    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/#setting-console>

Instead: 

    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/l10n/>
    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#l10n>

    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/l10n/#setting-console>
    <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#setting-console>

– where 'Localization - i18n/L10n Usage and Setup' is the title of the _chapter_ (not a section within a chapter).

Consider rewording the paragraph: 

> See the Console Setup section of Localization - i18n/L10n Usage and Setup, 
> kbdmap(1) in particular. 

(The Handbook is misleading, I'll raise a separate bug for this.)

----

See also: bug 260199, bug 263320 (likely overlap with FAQ).
